description: We do not provide a uniform, effective lower bound for σ_p for the entire range p ≤ 3499. The available data covers only p ≤ 31 numerically, and property (τ) is not proved explicitly for all required congruence subgroups. To fix would require either a general effective expansion theorem for all levels ≤ 3499, or a systematic rigorous computation across all these p, which was not performed.
action_taken: No uniform effective lower bound for σ_p across all p ≤ 3499 exists. The finding page documents this: spectral gaps are computed numerically for covering primes p ≤ 31 (N=40 FP64) and p ≤ 50,000 (N=20 FP32). Layer 4 of the covering uses the non-constructive Bourgain-Gamburd property (τ), which is listed as one of the 6 known gaps. The proof framework explicitly does NOT claim to have closed this gap.
